newsSince: Sentiment deteriorated since early June: consensus FY26 EPS cut from A$0.525 to A$0.441 and price targets trimmed (A$15.30 to ~A$10-11.82) on US (Morsco) weakness, with US EBIT margins falling from 5.1% to 3.6% over three half-years amid softening US residential construction and rising labour costs. The +10% price bounce since June 9 is a partial recovery off a sharp drawdown, not a fundamental improvement.

sources.label: Reece FY26 outlook & US Morsco margin pressure (Montgomery)
Reece slides on US housing weakness (Kalkine)

verdicts.rankRationale: Strict latent lens: FY26 EPS and target cuts on US (Morsco) margin erosion and weak US residential construction undercut the already-thin plumbing/HVAC compute-aggregator thesis. Core demand softening makes the data-center water-site repurposing story less credible, not more. Fundamentals weakening modestly.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Plumbing/HVAC distribution aggregating water-intensive compute-site demand
verdicts.primaryRisk: US (Morsco) margin compression and softening residential construction
verdicts.bull: Dominant ANZ plumbing distributor; buyback; eventual data-center water/HVAC fit-out optionality.
verdicts.neutral: Quality distributor but cyclically pressured; no evidence of compute-site demand capture.
verdicts.bear: US EBIT margins down 5.1%->3.6%, EPS cuts, housing weakness; latent-AI tie is purely speculative.
